Our Mission: Rebuilding Ukraine, One Community at a Time

Founded in 2022 by the Ukrainian-American diaspora, Go Ukraina is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit dedicated to delivering high-impact, sustainable infrastructure solutions to war-affected regions.

Who We Are

When the full-scale invasion began, millions of Ukrainians were left without access to basic necessities—power, shelter, and clean water. Go Ukraina was formed as a direct response to this humanitarian crisis.

We are not just delivering short-term aid; we are investing in the long-term resilience of Ukraine. By working directly with local municipalities and the State Agency for Reconstruction, we ensure our projects are targeted, efficient, and deeply aligned with the needs of the communities we serve.

Registered 501(c)(3) in California
100% of public donations go to programs
Direct partnerships with Ukrainian authorities
Led by diaspora business leaders
Our Leadership

Guided by experienced professionals committed to Ukraine's victory and recovery.

German Simakovski

President & Co-founder

Spearheading strategic partnerships and operations, driving our clean water and energy infrastructure deployments.

Olena Simakovski

Executive Director & Co-founder

Leading advocacy, fundraising initiatives, and the organization of the Ukraine Reconstruction Summit.

Adrien Tompert

Program Development Associate

A driven aspiring healthcare professional who has traveled to Ukraine to directly support humanitarian initiatives and fundraising efforts.

Nikol Bohach

Co-founder

Exemplifying the spirit of service and commitment to international solidarity, coordinating GoUkrainA's operations.

FLAGSHIP INITIATIVE
ReH2O: Solar-Powered Clean Water for Ukraine

Deploying independent, high-capacity reverse osmosis stations to communities whose water infrastructure has been destroyed by war.

The Crisis

Since the full-scale invasion, attacks on civilian infrastructure have decimated municipal water systems across Ukraine. The Ukraine water crisis is immense; millions lack reliable access to safe drinking water.

Without power, pumping stations fail. Without treatment facilities, disease spreads. Bottled water delivery is expensive, logistically complex, and unsustainable for long-term survival in conflict zones.

Our Solution
Solar Independence

Units operate entirely off-grid, ensuring continuous clean water even during total blackouts.

Reverse Osmosis

Industrial-grade purification removes toxins, heavy metals, and pathogens from compromised sources.

High Capacity

Each station can process enough daily drinking water to sustain an entire neighborhood or hospital.

See ReH2O In Action

Watch how our solar-powered purification units are engineered and deployed to bring clean water to Ukraine's most vulnerable communities.

ReH2O Field Deployment

Documentation of station delivery and its impact on the communities we serve.

ReH2O Station — 3D Overview

A detailed look at the engineering and components behind each solar-powered purification unit.

Station Delivery Gallery

From factory to field — photos documenting the manufacturing, transport, and deployment of our ReH2O purification stations.

The 150 Station Plan

The ReH2O model is proven. Now, we must scale. Go Ukraina aims to deploy 150 ReH2O stations across the most vulnerable regions of Ukraine over the next 24 months. We cannot do this without your support.

$80,000 Funds One Complete Station

A single donation of $80,000 covers the manufacturing, logistics, and installation of a full unit — each station produces 100,000 liters of clean filtered water every day, securing safe drinking water for thousands of people.

Partner With Us

Keeping the Lights On: Emergency Power Generators

Targeted strikes on Ukraine's energy grid mean winter is a weapon. We distribute industrial and portable generators to keep critical facilities operational.

Photo: Volunteers unloading generators in Kharkiv
The Impact

A single high-capacity generator can power an entire hospital wing—keeping ventilators running, operating rooms lit, and incubators warm during rolling blackouts.

Since 2022, we have delivered over 150 generators ranging from small residential units for isolated elderly populations to massive industrial units for medical facilities.

$100 = 1 Week of Fuel

Your donation keeps these engines running when they are needed most.

Power a Community

Help us purchase and transport the next shipment of generators before winter sets in.

HUMAN RIGHTS ADVOCACY
Standing Up for Every Ukrainian

Go Ukraina partners with the Office of the Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights to advance accountability, secure prisoner releases, and fight for the return of abducted children.

OFFICIAL PARTNERSHIP
Office of the Ombudsman of Ukraine

Go Ukraina has established a formal working partnership with the Office of the Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights, led by Commissioner Dmytro Lubinets.

This partnership enables us to serve as a transatlantic bridge — amplifying the Ombudsman's documentation of human rights violations to U.S. policy institutions, advocacy organizations, and international humanitarian partners.

Together, we coordinate outreach, policy briefings, and diplomatic engagement focused on two of the most urgent humanitarian crises of the war: the release of Ukrainian prisoners of war and the return of children unlawfully deported or forcibly transferred from Ukraine to Russia.

Our Advocacy Focus Areas

Guided by the Ombudsman's documented evidence, Go Ukraina advances four interlocking areas of advocacy.

Prisoners of War

Coordinating international advocacy and diplomatic engagement to secure the release of Ukrainian military personnel and civilians held as prisoners of war, drawing on documented evidence of torture and violations of the Geneva Conventions.

Abducted Children

Raising awareness and driving policy action on the systematic deportation and forced transfer of Ukrainian children to Russia — one of the most grave violations of international humanitarian law documented in this conflict.

Accountability & Justice

Supporting international legal mechanisms to document, preserve, and prosecute war crimes — ensuring that violations of international humanitarian and human rights law are met with accountability.

Transatlantic Engagement

Hosting high-level virtual policy briefings that connect the Ombudsman's office directly with U.S. congressional offices, advocacy organizations, and humanitarian partners to strengthen coordinated international action.

UPCOMING EVENT
High-Level Policy Briefing on Ukrainian POWs & Abducted Children

The Office of the Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights, in cooperation with Go Ukraina, is organizing a high-level virtual policy briefing with Commissioner Dmytro Lubinets.

This briefing will provide a direct institutional update on documented violations of international humanitarian and human rights law, the systematic deportation of Ukrainian children, and ongoing diplomatic and humanitarian coordination efforts.

Participants will hear directly from Commissioner Lubinets on the legal mechanisms being pursued, the current status of prisoner exchange negotiations, and the international accountability processes underway.

Format
Virtual Policy Briefing (Zoom)
Speaker
Dmytro Lubinets, Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights
Duration
45–60 minutes including moderated Q&A
Language
Ukrainian with English translation
Request to Participate
Support Our Advocacy Work

Every dollar supports our ability to maintain institutional partnerships, host policy briefings, and keep Ukraine's human rights crisis visible on the international stage.

Partner With Us

Ukraine Dreamzzz

An international sports and leadership initiative supporting young Ukrainian athletes in boxing, MMA, and kickboxing.

Ukraine Dreamzzz is an international sports and leadership initiative created to support young Ukrainians who have the talent, discipline, and drive to develop in combat sports such as boxing, MMA, and kickboxing.

What the program offers:

Professional training with experienced coaches
Safe housing and a structured sports environment
Opportunity to train in the USA (Miami)
Participation in competitions and international exposure
Support in developing discipline, leadership, and character
Assistance in attracting sponsors and long-term opportunities
Apply to the Program

Ukraine Reconstruction Summit

"From War to Renaissance"

September 2026
Washington D.C.
300+ Global Leaders
About the Summit

The Ukraine Reconstruction Summit is the premier gathering of investors, policymakers, NGO leaders, and the diaspora community. Our goal is to forge actionable partnerships that drive the physical and economic rebuilding of Ukraine.

Co-chaired by German Simakovski and Olena Simakovski, the 2025 event at the MGM National Harbor brought together over 260 leaders. The upcoming 2026 summit will expand on this foundation, focusing on energy resilience, clean water infrastructure, and foreign direct investment.

Get Notified for 2026

Join the mailing list to receive early access to tickets, speaker announcements, and sponsorship opportunities.

UKRAINE CRISIS
Addressing the Water Crisis in Ukraine: Go Ukraina and the WASH Cluster
German Simakovski
•
2024-12-05
•
3 min read
Photo: Addressing the Water Crisis in Ukraine: Go Ukraina and the WASH Cluster

The ongoing conflict in Ukraine has targeted essential civilian infrastructure, creating a severe crisis for millions of people. Among the most critical challenges is the lack of access to safe, clean drinking water.

The Scale of the Challenge

According to recent reports, continuous strikes on power grids and municipal water systems have left entire regions relying on unsafe water sources or costly bottled water. This not only poses immediate health risks but also strains local economies already devastated by war.

Our Partnership with the WASH Cluster

Go Ukraina has officially joined forces with the Global WASH Cluster to coordinate our response. By aligning our efforts with international organizations and local authorities, we ensure our ReH2O solar-powered water purification units are deployed where they are needed most.

Our pilot programs in the Kyiv region have already proven that decentralized, renewable-powered water stations can provide a resilient lifeline for communities facing regular grid failures.

Support Our Mission

Articles like this highlight the urgent need for action. 100% of your donation funds our programs directly.

The Humanitarian Crisis: Treatment of Ukrainian POWs and Civilians in Russian Detention
German Simakovski
•
2024-11-18
•
3 min read
Photo: The Humanitarian Crisis: Treatment of Ukrainian POWs and Civilians in Russian Detention

Recent investigations by international human rights organizations have brought to light the devastating conditions faced by Ukrainian Prisoners of War and arbitrarily detained civilians.

Systematic Violations

The United Nations has documented widespread instances of mistreatment, denial of medical care, and lack of communication with families. These actions stand in stark violation of the Geneva Conventions.

Our Advocacy Role

While Go Ukraina's primary mission involves physical reconstruction and infrastructure, we cannot ignore the human cost of this war. Through our diaspora network in the United States, we are actively working with policymakers to keep this issue at the forefront of diplomatic discussions.

Every voice matters. We urge our supporters to contact their representatives and demand continued pressure for the humane treatment and safe return of all detainees.

Support Our Mission

Articles like this highlight the urgent need for action. 100% of your donation funds our programs directly.

Go Ukraina & ReH2O Provide Clean Water to War-Affected Regions
German Simakovski
•
2024-10-03
•
4 min read
Photo: Go Ukraina & ReH2O Provide Clean Water to War-Affected Regions

Borodianka, a town heavily scarred by the early days of the full-scale invasion, is taking vital steps toward recovery. A major milestone in this journey is the restoration of safe drinking water.

Deploying the First ReH2O Station

In partnership with the State Agency for Reconstruction, Go Ukraina successfully installed our first high-capacity ReH2O reverse osmosis station. Capable of purifying thousands of liters per day, the unit operates independently of the central power grid thanks to integrated solar panels.

Community Impact

For the residents of Borodianka, this station means no longer relying on expensive deliveries or unsafe wells. It provides a reliable source of water for the local hospital, schools, and neighborhood centers.

This is just the beginning. Our goal is to scale this solution to 150 communities across Ukraine over the next 24 months. With your support, we can make clean water a reality for every Ukrainian.

Support Our Mission

Articles like this highlight the urgent need for action. 100% of your donation funds our programs directly.

Addressing the Water Crisis in Ukraine: Go Ukraina and the WASH Cluster

The ongoing conflict has severely disrupted clean water access across Ukraine. Go Ukraina is partnering with the WASH Cluster to deploy solar-powered purification stations.

The Impact of Your Gift
$25
